mnemonics: FINITE: Fits In Numbered, Identifiable Total Elements. INFINITE: Is Not Finite; It Never Ends.
keyPoints: A set is finite if its elements can be put into a one-to-one correspondence with the set {1, 2, ..., n} for some natural number n.
A set is infinite if it is not finite, meaning no such natural number n exists.
In a metric space, the concepts of openness, closedness, compactness, and convergence are deeply connected to whether sets are finite or infinite.
commonMistakes: Assuming all infinite sets are the same size (e.g., confusing countably infinite with uncountably infinite).
Believing a set must be finite to be compact; in metric spaces, closed and bounded sets can be infinite yet compact (Heine-Borel Theorem).
